extraction.one_line_summary: GIRMAY Biniam won from reduced bunch sprint after hot coastal stage split sprinters before final climb to Jesi.
tactical_patterns: EWAN Caleb dropped 76km out on moderate hills, pure sprinters inability to survive secondary climbs reveals vulnerability threshold.
YATES Simon attacking final 6km with FORMOLO Davide and CICCONE Giulio shows GC riders testing legs post-rest day.
CARAPAZ Richard and CARTHY Hugh probing final kilometer suggests GC teams willing to gamble seconds on rolling finishes.
NAESEN Lawrence, DE MARCHI Alessandro, BRÄNDLE Matthias break absorbed with 20km shows non-climber breakaways fail on technical finishes.

rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: Beat VAN DER POEL Mathieu head-to-head again in reduced sprint, proving day-one victory no fluke.
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Dropped 76km from finish on moderate climbs suggests poor form or heat vulnerability on rest-day-plus-one.
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: neutral: Required chase after mechanical but survived climbs; positioning better than pure sprinter cohort.
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: neutral: Attacked final 6km but refused to work with others, prioritizing sprint over breakaway commitment.
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: Initiating late attacks after losing 11 minutes stage-nine indicates hunting stage wins not GC recovery.
rider_signals.FOSS Tobias: neutral: Solo attack with 20km showed aggression but one-kilometer duration reveals insufficient form for hillier finishes.
rider_signals.POZZOVIVO Domenico: positive: Leading out sprint and pulling back attacks shows unexpected leadout skills from lightweight climber.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Juan Pedro: positive: Survived aggressive finale in pink despite doubts, suggests deeper resilience than assumed pre-race.
